title: vincetoxicum hirundinaria subsp. glaucum
intro: Vincetoxicum hirundinaria subsp. glaucum, also known as Perennial Larkspur or Woodlouse Hawk's Beard, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains toxic compounds, including vincetoxicine, which have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Vincetoxicum hirundinaria subsp. glaucum has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
